pattern: Whether a day qualifies depends only on how far back a non-increasing run extends and how far forward a non-decreasing run extends, and both of those can be precomputed once in a single left-to-right and right-to-left pass instead of rechecked per day.
hints: Checking the full window before and after every single day from scratch is wasteful -- the 'how far does this non-increasing run reach' question has a lot of overlap between neighboring days.

Build one array where entry i counts how many days in a row up to i have been non-increasing (comparing each day to the one right before it), and a second array counting how many days in a row from i onward are non-decreasing.

A day qualifies exactly when both of those run-lengths at that day are at least `time` long. Handle `time = 0` as a special case where every day automatically qualifies since there's nothing to check on either side.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, exactly two good days form a valley
statement example, time = 0 means every day qualifies
statement example, strictly increasing guards, no good day
time so large no day has enough room on either side
all equal values, every interior day within range qualifies since non-increasing and non-decreasing both hold trivially
a single dip in the middle surrounded by flat plateaus
strictly decreasing then strictly increasing, valley sits exactly at the minimum
two separate valleys, both should qualify independently

stress.description: random security levels with a random window size, sometimes larger than any valid position
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    security = [rng.randint(0, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    time = rng.randint(0, n)
    return [security, time]
